raw_cv_text:
Carl Corp
mperial College London 2022 – 2026
MEng Computing (Machine Learning)
Currently in Year 3: 68% (2:1)
SKILLS
Python, Java, C++, C, Front-End (Flutter, React, SvelteKit, Next.js), Javascript,
CSS (Tailwind and Vanilla), Haskell, Git, Linux, GDB, Docker, CI/CD
EXPERIENCE
Software Engineering Intern 2024
Great Wave AI - Multi Agent RAG Startup
Developed full-stack platform features with Python (Flask), SQLAlchemy, fastAPI and SvelteKit, all code
•
deployed to production
Implemented instruction versioning and draft support, optimising for read performance through schema
•
design
Worked with Sales to rapidly develop POCs for potential clients including HMRC, CGI, iterating our
•
product using feedback ( new features and API endpoints personally implemented )
Expanded userbase by building custom connectors for no-code platforms like Zapier
•
PROJECTS
Medical GenAI with HeartFlow present
Full Stack Group Project, Spring
Full stack application to enhance data collection for image-generation AI
•
Currently working with HeartFlow and Dr Ben Glocker
•
Bloom - AI-based Language Learning App (Flutter, Firebase) - Group Project 2024
Full Stack Group Project, Summer (78%)
Created a language learning app, using generative AI to tailor around use cases.
•
Targeting languages that are lost from the children of immigrant parents (Urdu,
Arabic, etc)
Conversation feature with speech or text, with levels of difficulty and specific
•
scenarios available, users are able to get hints on how to respond.
Reading feature generates text of variable complexity, and provides help in the
•
form of generated context-relevant flash cards.
WACC Compiler (Scala) - Group Project 2024
Compilers Group Project, Spring (80%)
Implementing full compiler for a while-based language in using CI/CD.
•
Parsing/AST generation and semantic analysis
•
Backend (AST to x86 Assembly) (all tests passed)
•
Extensions: Global monomorphic type inference, Peephole optimisations, REPL
•
PintOS Operating Systems (C) - Group Project 2023
Operating Systems Group Project, Winter (86%)
Implemented scheduling/memory features in a barebones operating system
•
Scheduling with priority donations and Multi-Level feedback queue system
•
Support for concurrent system calls with process forking
•
Virtual Memory support, using a modified second chance algorithm
•
Raspberry Pi Assembler and Emulator - Group Project
Computing Practical Project, Summer 2023 (100%)
Developed an AArch64 emulator that would simulate the execution of a binary
•
file on a Raspberry Pi in C
Developed an assembler that translated AArch64 source files into executable
•
